title: When date arrive delete/move all subitems
excerpt:
Hi, 
Is it possible to create an automation that when date arrive delete/move all subitems? 
thanks.

posts.author: eranggg (eran)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi,

Is it possible to create an automation that when date arrive delete/move all subitems?

thanks.

posts.author: CharlotteK (Charlotte Kirkham)
posts.cookedHtml:
eran:

Is it possible to create an automation that when date arrive delete/move all subitems?

Hey Eran! This exact flow isn’t possible but we’d love to better understand your workflow needs and use case so we can suggest a workaround. Feel free to reach out to us here or on Twitter to discuss further!

posts.author: Fantasy-Media-Team (Fantasy Media Team)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @eranggg using subitem automations you can accomplish that with a slight workaround. See below →

You can use this automation to set status to something when date arrives →

Screenshot 2024-04-24 at 15.00.371770×268 17.5 KB

And then use this to move →

Screenshot 2024-04-24 at 15.01.262084×298 25.3 KB

Or use this to archive →

Screenshot 2024-04-24 at 15.01.561950×330 29.4 KB

Hope that helps!

posts.author: MsquareAutomation (Msquare Automation Solutions)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @eranggg ,

This automation is achievable through Make.com ,
Moving a sub-item to another item isn’t directly possible in Monday.com Automation ,

However you can work around it using Make.com automation features.

Instead of moving a sub-item, you can create a new sub-item in another item and then delete the original sub-item using Make.com Automation. This process allows you to achieve the desired outcome effectively .
